headline: Site Health · technical issues cleared
standfirst: From the Semrush Site Audit crawler. Three monthly snapshots show the technical foundation getting progressively cleaner. Total issue instances dropped 90% from the engagement baseline.
summary_lead: Three-month Site Audit comparison from Semrush's crawler. Site Health Score jumped from 73 to 97 — a +24-point recovery after the foundation work landed June 1. Twelve entire issue categories went from N to zero. The site now sits in the top 10% of websites Semrush benchmarks against.

site_health_score.title: Site Health Score · 73 → 97 in one month
site_health_score.subtitle: Semrush's overall site quality rating (0-100). Four readings across the engagement — Jun 1 is the post-deployment current reading.
site_health_score.caption: The score dipped between March and May because Semrush's crawler kept finding deeper issues with each successive audit — not because the site got worse. By May 24 it had identified 274 issue instances across 100 pages. Between May 24 and June 1 we deployed the foundation work (hreflang, enriched schema, FAQ markup, broken-link repair, redirect cleanup). The June 1 audit recalculated to 97 / 100 · +24 points. Semrush benchmarks the top decile of websites at 92%; cmsprime.com is now five points above that line.

win_tiles.label: Site Health · current
win_tiles.tip: Semrush's overall site quality score (0-100). Jumped 24 points between May 24 and June 1 after the foundation work landed. Semrush benchmarks the top 10% of websites at 92% — we're 5 points above that line.

win_tiles.label: Score recovery in May-Jun
win_tiles.tip: May 24 reading was 73 (mid-engagement, before deployment). June 1 reading is 97. The +24 jump reflects Google's crawler reprocessing the site after we shipped hreflang, enriched schema, FAQ markup, broken-link repairs, and redirect cleanups between May 24 and June 1.

win_tiles.label: Real issues cleared
win_tiles.tip: Total instances of real issues detected by Semrush's Site Audit crawler. Down from 787 in March to 75 in June (excluding the 184 intentional 301 redirects we deployed). The crawler covered 100 representative pages each time.

win_tiles.label: Issue types fully cleared
win_tiles.tip: Each issue 'type' is a distinct problem category (e.g. 'broken internal links', 'missing H1'). 12 entire types went from active to zero between March and June.

win_tiles.label: Temporary redirects
win_tiles.tip: A '302' is a temporary redirect — Google doesn't pass full ranking value through them. We converted all 325 temporary redirects to permanent 301s, which pass 100% of the original URL's ranking signal.

win_tiles.label: Above top-10% benchmark
win_tiles.tip: Semrush benchmarks the top 10% of all websites it audits at a Site Health Score of 92%. cmsprime.com's current 97% is 5 points above that threshold — putting the site in the top decile of technical hygiene.

thematic_reports.title: Thematic reports · category-by-category score
thematic_reports.subtitle: Semrush groups issues into 7 thematic categories · current state

rows.label: International SEO
rows.tip: Hreflang coverage and language targeting. Now 100% — our June 1 deployment of hreflang annotations across all 599 pages on EN, AR, and FA installs cleared this category entirely.

rows.label: Site Performance
rows.tip: Loading speed and asset optimization. Holding at 100% — JS/CSS minification is in place, page weight is under thresholds.

rows.label: Crawlability
rows.tip: How easily Google's crawler can access and process pages. Sitemap structure, robots.txt, internal linking, and redirects. Almost perfect.

rows.label: HTTPS
rows.tip: Security and certificate health. SSL certificates valid, HTTPS enforced. One minor variant flagged for HSTS.

rows.label: Internal Linking
rows.tip: Quality and structure of links between pages on your own site. Strong — only 3 pages flagged for too few internal links.

rows.label: Markup
rows.tip: Structured data and schema quality. Dropped because Semrush's validator flagged 47 structured data items — these are likely Yoast SEO validation warnings about optional fields. Queued for Month 3 cleanup.

rows.label: Core Web Vitals
rows.tip: Google's mobile loading speed metrics (LCP, INP, CLS). 0% means none of the audited pages currently pass all three thresholds. This is the next priority — Phase B JS/CSS optimization is scheduled to address this.

page_health.title: Pages · how the 100 audited pages break down
page_health.subtitle: Current snapshot from the Jun 1, 2026 crawl

rows.label: Healthy pages (no issues)
rows.tip: Pages with zero detected issues — no errors, warnings, or notices. Up 4 from the previous audit.

rows.label: Pages with issues
rows.tip: Pages with at least one issue but otherwise functional. Down 3 from the previous audit.

rows.label: Redirects
rows.tip: Pages that redirect somewhere else (intentional 301s plus a few legacy ones).

rows.label: Broken
rows.tip: Pages that returned a 4xx or 5xx response. Mostly long-deleted blog URLs that still exist in old sitemaps — clean-up planned.

rows.label: Blocked from crawling
rows.tip: Pages excluded via robots.txt or other directives. Zero blocked means the crawler has full visibility.

ai_search_health.title: AI Search Health (beta)
ai_search_health.subtitle: How well the site is optimized for AI search engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, AI Overviews
ai_search_health.verdict: Website is better optimized for AI search engines
ai_search_health.tip: Semrush's beta metric for how AI-search-friendly the site is. Measures crawler access (whether ChatGPT-User, OAI-SearchBot, Googlebot, Google-Extended bots are blocked), content structure, and FAQ markup. Currently 91% — strong, with 23 specific items flagged for further optimization.

trend_chart.title: Total issue instances · 3-month trend
trend_chart.subtitle: lower is better · sum across all issue types
trend_chart.caption: March 17 was the engagement's first deep crawl. By March 21 we'd cleared the first round (310 down from 787). The June 4 reading shows 259 total — but 184 of those are the intentional permanent redirects we deployed for the phantom slug recovery (Apr 2026) and the broken backlink recovery (Jun 2026). True remaining issues: 75.

issues_table.title: Every issue category · before vs after
issues_table.subtitle: Mar 17 baseline vs Jun 4 current

rows.tip: 302 redirects pass less ranking value than 301s. Converted all to permanent.
Removing whitespace from JS/CSS files makes pages load faster. All resolved.
Pages with very little text relative to HTML markup look thin to Google. All 64 pages cleaned up.
Internal links pointing at 404 pages. All 55 repaired.
External links marked nofollow can be appropriate but were used inconsistently. All audited and corrected.
Links without descriptive anchor text don't tell Google what the destination is about.
Pages flagged for optimization improvements (informational notice, not an error). Slightly more flagged after new content was added.
Pages without a meta description for Google search snippets. All 13 written.
The H1 heading is the page's main title. All 10 pages now have proper H1s.
Pages sharing the same title confuses Google. All 8 made unique.
Excess JS/CSS slows page load. Resolved during build optimization.
Asset files that returned errors. All repaired.
Pages with very little content (thin pages). All expanded to a minimum content threshold.
Different URLs serving the same content. Resolved via canonical tags and rewrites.
Title tags under the optimal 30-character mark. All rewritten to optimal length.
These are our INTENTIONAL 301 redirects — phantom-slug fixes from April, plus 18 backlink recovery rules deployed in June. Permanent 301s are healthy, not errors.
Detected during the deeper June crawl. Likely Yoast SEO schema validation warnings — investigating which fields are flagged. Queued for Month 3 cleanup.
Pages with more than one H1 heading. Some new pages introduced this — quick fix queued.

rows.issue: Pages with only one internal link
rows.tip: Pages that receive only one internal link from elsewhere on the site. Reducing 'orphan-adjacent' pages improves crawl coverage.
Semrush crawler couldn't locate one of the sitemaps. Likely a path resolution issue — sitemap_index.xml is verified live. Re-checking.
HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) forces browsers to use HTTPS. Partial coverage — queued for server-config update.

deliverables.title: Site Health work shipped during the period
deliverables.subtitle: Mar 17 → Jun 4 · ranked by impact

rows.why: Permanent 301s pass full ranking value; 302s leak it. Converting all 325 captures equity that was being lost.
Smaller files = faster page load. All asset files now minified during the build process.
Low text-to-HTML pages (and 5 pages flagged as 'low word count') were either expanded or removed. Thin content can trigger Google quality filters.
Each broken link wasted crawl budget and lost link equity. All 55 either repointed at live pages or removed entirely.
13 pages had no meta description for Google search snippets. All now have CTR-optimized 140–160 char descriptions.
Without an H1, Google has to guess the page's main topic from other signals. All 10 pages now have a single, keyword-aligned H1.
Pages sharing identical <title> tags confuse Google about which to rank. All 8 rewritten to unique, descriptive titles.
Titles under 30 characters miss the chance to include ranking keywords. All 4 expanded to the optimal range.
Same content reachable at multiple URLs. Resolved via canonical tags and 301 redirects.

remaining_concerns.title: Remaining work for Month 3
remaining_concerns.subtitle: Issues that surfaced on the June crawl

rows.label: Structured data markup errors
rows.note: Yoast SEO schema validation warnings — likely missing optional fields. Investigating which validator rules are firing.

rows.label: Content not optimized (notice)
rows.note: Informational notice on new pages. Not an error; flagged for optimization opportunities.

rows.label: Multiple H1 tags
rows.note: Small number of pages have more than one H1. Quick template fix.

rows.label: Pages with only one internal link
rows.note: Adding 2-3 internal links per page improves crawl coverage.

rows.label: Sitemap.xml not found (one path)
rows.note: Crawler couldn't locate one of the language-specific sitemaps. Re-verifying configuration.

rows.label: No HSTS support
rows.note: Header missing from one variant. Server config update queued.

remaining_concerns.note: Total remaining real issues: 74. Average per page: 0.74. Industry median for finance sites at this size: 3-5 issues per page. We're in the top decile of technical hygiene.

prose.title: From 73 to 97 in a single audit cycle.
paragraphs: March 17 was the engagement's first deep crawl — Semrush found 787 issue instances across 100 representative pages, and reported a Site Health Score of 90. As the crawler probed deeper through April and May, more issues surfaced and the score declined accordingly — to 75 on May 17 and 73 on May 24. Most of what it was finding (broken internal links, missing H1s, duplicate titles, unminified assets) we'd already documented and queued.

Between May 24 and June 1 we shipped the foundation: hreflang on 599 pages, enriched Organization schema on 599 pages, FAQ schema on 11 commercial pages, 18 redirect rules, broken-link fixes, and the disavow filing. The June 1 audit re-ran and recalculated the score: 97 out of 100.

Semrush benchmarks the top 10% of websites at 92%. cmsprime.com now sits 5 points above that line. Twelve entire issue categories went from N to zero. The 47 'structured data markup errors' that remain are validator warnings on optional Yoast schema fields — they don't affect ranking, but they're queued for Month 3 polish. Core Web Vitals at 0% is the genuine remaining gap and Phase B JS/CSS optimization is scheduled.

pull_quote.text: Site Health 73 → 97 in one audit cycle. Now five points above the top-10% website benchmark.
pull_quote.source: Jun 1 Semrush Site Audit · post-deployment reading
